parameters.jsCode:
const CHANNEL = 'C0ADHLAPBPD';
const clients = [
  {
    name: 'Acumen Billing',
    industry: 'Dental Billing & RCM',
    searchQueries: [
      'DSO acquisition OR dental practice merger OR dental group private equity deal',
      'dental insurance reimbursement change OR Medicare dental coverage OR Medicaid dental policy',
      'dental billing technology OR dental RCM automation OR dental AI revenue cycle'
    ],
    competitors: 'eAssist, DCS, Dental Butler, Overjet, DentalXChange, Dentrix Ascend, Vyne Dental',
    personas: 'Dentist-owners, Office Managers, VP Revenue Cycle at DSOs',
    slackChannel: CHANNEL
  },
  {
    name: 'Veridia Health',
    industry: 'Medical Device Compliance / RegTech',
    searchQueries: [
      'FDA warning letter medical device OR FDA inspection findings OR medical device recall',
      'QMSR compliance medical device OR ISO 13485 update OR EU MDR enforcement',
      '510k clearance OR FDA premarket approval OR medical device regulatory technology'
    ],
    competitors: 'Greenlight Guru, Qualio, MasterControl, Veeva Vault, Arena Solutions, Dot Compliance, Rimsys',
    personas: 'VP Quality & Regulatory, Head of Software Engineering, RA Directors',
    slackChannel: CHANNEL
  },
  {
    name: 'Lucidia Health',
    industry: 'Digital Mental Health Diagnostics',
    searchQueries: [
      'mental health startup funding OR digital psychiatry acquisition OR behavioral health platform deal',
      'ADHD telehealth regulation OR DEA prescribing rule online OR telemental health policy',
      'university mental health crisis OR college counseling demand OR student mental health investment'
    ],
    competitors: 'Cerebral, Done Health, Talkiatry, Rula, Headway, BetterHelp, Brightside Health',
    personas: 'Directors Student Health, VP Benefits, Psychiatry Practice Owners',
    slackChannel: CHANNEL
  },
  {
    name: 'GiveLoop',
    industry: 'Nonprofit Fundraising / P2P Giving Platform',
    searchQueries: [
      'nonprofit fundraising platform acquired OR donor management software funding OR giving tech merger',
      'peer-to-peer fundraising results OR charity endurance event donations OR marathon fundraising record',
      'online donation growth trends OR digital giving report OR nonprofit technology adoption'
    ],
    competitors: 'Classy, GoFundMe Pro, JustGiving, Blackbaud, RallyUp, Givebutter, Bloomerang',
    personas: 'Nonprofit Executive Directors, Event Fundraising Managers, Foundation Development Officers',
    slackChannel: CHANNEL
  },
  {
    name: 'Encore Entertainment Services',
    industry: 'Entertainment Payroll & Production Software',
    searchQueries: [
      'IATSE contract OR SAG-AFTRA agreement OR film crew union wage negotiation',
      'film production spending OR studio production budget OR streaming content investment slate',
      'film tax credit OR production incentive state program OR entertainment payroll technology'
    ],
    competitors: 'Entertainment Partners, Wrapbook, Cast & Crew, GreenSlate, Media Services, Sargent-Disc',
    personas: 'Line Producers, Production Accountants, Studio VP Production Finance, UPMs',
    slackChannel: CHANNEL
  },
  {
    name: 'Sourcely AI',
    industry: 'AI Product Sourcing / Global Manufacturing',
    searchQueries: [
      'US tariff China manufacturing OR trade policy import duty OR supply chain reshoring nearshoring',
      'AI product sourcing platform OR automated supplier discovery OR manufacturing procurement technology funding',
      'ecommerce brand sourcing OR DTC supply chain cost OR global factory quality control automation'
    ],
    competitors: 'Pietra, Sourcify, Alibaba, Faire, Zignify, Novaeo, Thomas, MFG.com',
    personas: 'DTC Brand Founders, VP Supply Chain, eCommerce Operations Leads, Head of Procurement, CPOs',
    slackChannel: CHANNEL
  }
];
return clients.map(c => ({ json: c }));

values.content:
You are a senior analyst at a B2B intelligence agency. You write dense, factual weekly briefings.

CRITICAL RULES:
1. ONLY use facts from the provided source articles. NEVER invent statistics, dates, company names, or deal sizes.
2. Ground claims in specifics (names, numbers, dates) but prioritize readable flow over fact-density. Not every sentence needs a statistic.
3. If an article date is before January 2026, explicitly note "reported in [month year]" — do NOT pretend it's current.
4. If the articles are irrelevant to the client's business, say so directly. Do not force connections.
5. Output RAW HTML only. No markdown. No code fences. No ``` anywhere.

=Write a weekly intelligence briefing as RAW HTML for this client. CLIENT: {{ $json.name }} INDUSTRY: {{ $json.industry }} BUYER PERSONAS: {{ $json.personas }} COMPETITORS TO WATCH: {{ $json.competitors }} SOURCE ARTICLES (use ONLY these): {{ $json.articlesText }} OUTPUT THIS EXACT HTML STRUCTURE:

Week in Review: [Write 1-2 flowing paragraphs as a cohesive executive summary. Open with the most significant development, then contextualize with supporting facts. Connect ideas with transitions—don't list facts sequentially. Remember this is a summary of what follows, so phrase accordingly. If no relevant news exists, write one sentence: "Limited relevant coverage this week."]

Key Developments

[Write 1-4 developments ONLY if articles are relevant and from the past 7 days. If no relevant 2026 news exists, skip straight to Competitive Intelligence and write nothing here.]

[Headline format: "Company does X - Month Day, Year" — ALWAYS end with the article date]

[2-4 sentences with specific details from the article. Don't pad.] (Source)

Competitive Intelligence

[2-3 sentences. Only mention competitors if they appear in the articles. If none appear, OMIT this entire section.]

Buyer Signals

[2-3 sentences. Connect article findings to the listed buyer personas. If articles are too thin to draw meaningful connections, OMIT this entire section.]

STRICT REQUIREMENTS: - TARGET: 400-700 words (shorter is fine on light weeks) - Source links: (Source) using ACTUAL URLs from articles - If fewer than 2 relevant articles, keep it brief—don't manufacture content - BANNED WORDS: synergy, innovative, cutting-edge, capitalize, holistic, game-changer, rapidly evolving, streamline, robust, optimize, landscape, paradigm, significant, noteworthy, excited - Raw HTML only — no markdown, no code blocks
You write ultra-concise intelligence digests. Each bullet is ONE specific fact. No adjectives. No filler. No commentary.

=Write exactly 2 bullet points per client. Each bullet: 8-12 words MAX. One fact. No context, no explanation.

{{ $json.tldrInput }}

FORMAT:

WISDOM
• [Fact with company name, number, or date]
• [Different fact]

INFERA HEALTH
• [Fact]
• [Fact]

[Continue for ALL clients]

RULES:
- Exactly 2 bullets per client
- 8-12 words per bullet MAX
- Start each bullet with WHO or WHAT (company/entity name)
- If no articles: "• No relevant news this week."
- Plain text only. No markdown.
